commands.review_summary:
The PR adds durable verdict marker freshness checks to the event publish path and only marks event re-review runs Complete when the live marker matches the current PR head SHA.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main reports Complete from step outcomes alone, and the linked report describes repeated runs where the durable verdict marker stayed on the old head SHA.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is maintainer-authored and has no narrow code defect for an automated repair pass; maintainers should decide whether to accept the workflow status semantics and merge normally.

target.title: fix(repair): gate event re-review completion on verdict marker freshness
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem:** Event-driven `@clawsweeper re-review` reports `State: Complete` even when the durable verdict marker is never refreshed to the new head SHA. Labels (e.g. `status: ⏳ waiting on author`) then stay stuck and bury the PR.

**Root cause:** The `Mark re-review complete` step in `.github/workflows/sweep.yml` set `state="Complete"` purely from the three step exit outcomes (review / publish / route all `success`). It never verified that the durable `clawsweeper-verdict` marker advanced to the PR's current head. When apply ran against a report whose `pull_head_sha` was not refreshed (the review produced no fresh artifact, or the snapshot resolved to `missing` / `no changes`), the marker stayed pinned to the old SHA — yet the status still read `Complete`. This is a truthfulness gap in the completion gate, not the "identical body → hash-skip sync" path hypothesized in the issue: the marker SHA is part of the hashed body and `apply-artifacts` forces a reference sync (`review_comment_id: unknown`), so a fresh artifact always syncs; the marker only stays stale when no fresh artifact replaced the report.

**Fix:** After a successful publish, `publish-event-result` compares the live durable verdict marker SHA against the current PR head and records `marker_fresh=1|0`. The completion gate now reports `Complete` only when `marker_fresh=1`; otherwise it reports `Incomplete` with a detail explaining the marker did not advance, so operators know to re-run instead of trusting a false `Complete`.

### What changed
- `src/repair/comment-router-core.ts`: two pure exported helpers — `durableVerdictMarkerSha()` (reuses the existing marker parser) and `eventReviewMarkerFreshness()` (reuses the existing `reviewedHeadShaBlockReason` to compare marker SHA vs current head; non-PR items are vacuously fresh).
- `src/repair/publish-event-result.ts`: fetches the live durable review comment + current PR head, writes `marker_fresh` to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`. Fully guarded — it never fails the publish step and defaults to `0` (unverified) on any error.
- `.github/workflows/sweep.yml`: completion gate reports `Complete` only on `marker_fresh=1`, otherwise `Incomplete`.
- `test/repair/comment-router-core.test.ts`: 5 focused cases (fresh / stale-old-head / no-marker / non-PR / marker-sha parse).

### What did NOT change
- `src/clawsweeper.ts` is untouched (no change to the review / marker generation path).
- No config surface, no plugin/SDK surface, no dependency changes.
- The normal path is unchanged: when the marker does advance, the run still reports `Complete`. Only the reported buggy scenario now reports `Incomplete` instead of a false `Complete`.

### Scope
This makes the completion gate truthful and surfaces the stale-marker condition rather than force-refreshing the marker (which would mean touching the review/marker generation path). Hence `Refs #170` rather than `Fixes`: the deeper "why did this particular re-review produce no fresh artifact" can be diagnosed per-case once the gate stops masking it.

## Verification
- `pnpm run test:repair` (Node 24): 398 → 403 pass / 0 fail (+5 new tests, no regression).
- `pnpm run check` (Node 24): exit 0 — `build:all`, `lint`, `test:unit`, `test:repair`, `test:coverage`, and `format:check` ("All matched files use the correct format") all green.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/issues/170
